title: Digitaria diagonalis
uses: Digitaria diagonalis, also known as finger grass, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing swelling. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its purported ability to soothe the stomach and intestines. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections and promote healing. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Digitaria diagonalis.

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
